identity.name: SuiNS (Sui Name Service)
identity.oneLiner: The naming service for Sui: register human-readable .sui names that map to addresses and are owned as NFTs.

sources.quote: Sui Name Service (SuiNS) is a decentralized naming service on the Sui blockchain that enables users to replace complex wallet addresses with human-readable names.
Represented as NFTs, SuiNS identifiers are stored in the user's wallet, ensuring that ownership benefits, granted to Sui objects, also apply to SuiNS identifiers.

entities.name: SuiNS (Sui Name Service)
entities.description: A decentralized naming service on Sui for registering human-readable names.

sources.quote: Sui Name Service (SuiNS) is a decentralized naming service on the Sui blockchain that enables users to replace complex wallet addresses with human-readable names.

entities.name: Sui blockchain
entities.description: The blockchain that SuiNS issues identifiers on.

sources.quote: The SuiNS name service lets Sui users own a human-readable identifier and associate it with a Sui account.

entities.name: .sui identifier (e.g. alice.sui)
entities.description: A human-readable identifier SuiNS issues on Sui, such as alice.sui.

sources.quote: As a name service platform, SuiNS issues identifiers on Sui, like alice.sui.

entities.name: NFT-based name ownership
entities.description: SuiNS identifiers are represented as NFTs stored in the user's wallet.

sources.quote: Represented as NFTs, SuiNS identifiers are stored in the user's wallet, ensuring that ownership benefits, granted to Sui objects, also apply to SuiNS identifiers.

entities.name: Subnames
entities.description: Nested names created under a parent name at no cost.

sources.quote: Subnames are nested names under a parent name.

entities.name: Node subnames
entities.description: Subnames with an associated NFT that unlocks extra features.

sources.quote: Node subnames: Similar to regular names, they have an NFT associated with them that gives access to all extra features (personalization, avatars, and so on).

entities.name: Leaf subnames
entities.description: Subnames without an NFT, configured by the parent.

sources.quote: They do not have an NFT associated with them, so the parent controls their configuration.

entities.name: Avatars
entities.description: Personalization that associates an avatar with a name.

sources.quote: After you register a name, you can set the avatar to associate with that name.

entities.name: IPFS integration (sui.id)
entities.description: Connect a SuiNS name to an IPFS-hosted site via a sui.id address.

sources.quote: Using IPFS, you can create a `sui.id` address connected to your SuiNS name to claim a spot on the decentralized web.

entities.name: SuiNS smart contracts
entities.description: The repository holding the SuiNS core and system contract packages.

sources.quote: This repository holds the SuiNS contracts (both the core and all system packages).

entities.name: Move Registry (MVR)
entities.description: A uniform naming service for packages in the Sui ecosystem.

sources.quote: Move Registry (MVR, pronounced mover) provides a uniform naming service for interacting and building with packages from the Sui ecosystem.

sources.quote: Unclaimed SuiNS identifiers are priced based on the number of characters: those with five characters or more cost 20 SUI, those with four characters cost 100 SUI

stats.label: Move share of the contracts codebase

sources.quote: Move 70.5%
Sui Name Service (SuiNS) is a decentralized naming service on the Sui blockchain that enables users to replace complex wallet addresses with human-readable names.
As a name service platform, SuiNS issues identifiers on Sui, like alice.sui.
Represented as NFTs, SuiNS identifiers are stored in the user's wallet, ensuring that ownership benefits, granted to Sui objects, also apply to SuiNS identifiers.
Subnames are nested names under a parent name.
Node subnames: Similar to regular names, they have an NFT associated with them that gives access to all extra features (personalization, avatars, and so on).
Leaf subnames: These are subnames that are created under a node subname.
After you register a name, you can set the avatar to associate with that name.
Using IPFS, you can create a `sui.id` address connected to your SuiNS name to claim a spot on the decentralized web.
This repository holds the SuiNS contracts (both the core and all system packages).
A MVR name consists of a SuiNS name and a package name, in the format `<suins_name>/<pkg_name>`.
provides a uniform naming service for interacting and building with packages from the Sui ecosystem.

questions.question: What is SuiNS?
questions.answer: SuiNS (Sui Name Service) is a decentralized naming service on the Sui blockchain that lets users replace complex wallet addresses with human-readable names.

sources.quote: Sui Name Service (SuiNS) is a decentralized naming service on the Sui blockchain that enables users to replace complex wallet addresses with human-readable names.

questions.question: How do SuiNS names work and who owns them?
questions.answer: SuiNS issues identifiers like alice.sui that link to a Sui account; they are represented as NFTs stored in the user's wallet.

sources.quote: Represented as NFTs, SuiNS identifiers are stored in the user's wallet, ensuring that ownership benefits, granted to Sui objects, also apply to SuiNS identifiers.

questions.question: How much does it cost to register a SuiNS name?
questions.answer: Names are priced by character count for one year of registration: 20 SUI for five or more characters, 100 SUI for four characters, and 500 SUI for three characters.

sources.quote: Unclaimed SuiNS identifiers are priced based on the number of characters: those with five characters or more cost 20 SUI, those with four characters cost 100 SUI

questions.question: How do I register a SuiNS name?
questions.answer: Use the SuiNS Dashboard link in the top nav to go to SuiNS, then search for a name between 3 and 63 characters and register it.

sources.quote: Type a name of interest in the search field between 3 and 63 characters, inclusive.

questions.question: What are subnames, and how do node and leaf subnames differ?
questions.answer: Subnames are nested names created under a parent at no cost; node subnames have an NFT that unlocks extra features, while leaf subnames have no NFT and are configured by the parent.

sources.quote: Subnames are nested names under a parent name.
They do not have an NFT associated with them, so the parent controls their configuration.

questions.question: How long can I keep a SuiNS name and what happens when it expires?
questions.answer: Registration is for one year, with a 30-day grace period to renew, and names can be renewed for up to 5 years.

sources.quote: You have a 30-day grace period within which you can renew the registration.
You can renew, or extend the expiration date, for any of your registered names for up to 5 years.

questions.question: Can I connect my SuiNS name to a decentralized website?
questions.answer: Yes. Using IPFS you can create a sui.id address connected to your SuiNS name to claim a spot on the decentralized web, mapped via a content identifier (CID).

sources.quote: Using IPFS, you can create a `sui.id` address connected to your SuiNS name to claim a spot on the decentralized web.
You need a content identifier (CID) to map your SuiNS name to your IPFS asset.

questions.question: What is Move Registry (MVR) and how does it relate to SuiNS?
questions.answer: MVR is a uniform naming service for Sui packages; an MVR name consists of a SuiNS name and a package name in the format <suins_name>/<pkg_name>.

sources.quote: Move Registry (MVR, pronounced mover) provides a uniform naming service for interacting and building with packages from the Sui ecosystem.
A MVR name consists of a SuiNS name and a package name, in the format `<suins_name>/<pkg_name>`.

questions.question: Where is the SuiNS code and how are admin operations handled?
questions.answer: The suins-contracts repository holds the SuiNS core and system packages, with admin actions built as multi-sig operations.

sources.quote: This repository holds the SuiNS contracts (both the core and all system packages).
different actions we want to build for various multi-sig operations
